563.alimentacao: Algae, pellets, plants, and dead fish.

570.alimentacao: Algae, pellets, plants, and dead fish.

571.alimentacao: Pellets, algae, decomposing leaves, dead fish.

573.outrasInformacoes: Similar to the Bladder Snail (Physa) and easily confused with it. They are pulmonate and hermaphroditic, capable of self-fertilization.

576.outrosNome: Tylomelania sp. Orange Orange Rock Snail

579.outrasInformacoes: Despite its Asian origin, it can be found on several continents, where it is considered an invasive pest.

582.alimentacao: Pellets, copepods, aiptasia, fish parasites, etc.

583.alimentacao: Pellets, fireworms, brine shrimp, shrimp pieces, etc.
583.comportamento: Can be aggressive toward other shrimp, including its own species.
583.diformismoSexual: Females are larger than males and are easily distinguished during breeding season by the blue eggs attached to their pleopods.

584.alimentacao: Pellets, fireworms, brine shrimp, shrimp pieces, etc.
584.comportamento: Can be aggressive toward smaller shrimp.

585.comportamento: Peaceful, cleans parasites from fish. Lives in pairs or groups.
585.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 40 liters. One of the most useful invertebrates in a reef tank.

586.comportamento: Peaceful, lives in symbiosis with watchman gobies. Produces a snapping sound with its claw.
586.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 40 liters. Forms a fascinating pair with gobies.

587.comportamento: Peaceful, dances by swaying its body. Lives among anemones.
587.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 20 liters. One of the smallest marine shrimp.

588.comportamento: Extremely aggressive predator. Has the fastest punch in the animal kingdom. Can break glass.
588.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 80 liters. DEDICATED tank ONLY. Can break thin aquarium glass.

589.comportamento: Peaceful with fish, but may nip at soft corals and zoanthids.
589.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 40 liters. NOT reef safe.

590.comportamento: Peaceful, excellent algae eater. Part of the clean-up crew.
590.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 20 liters. Keep extra shells available.

592.comportamento: Peaceful, electric blue legs, eats algae.
592.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 20 liters. One of the most beautiful hermit crabs.

593.comportamento: Peaceful, excellent for controlling bubble algae (Valonia).
593.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 40 liters. Reef safe.

594.comportamento: Peaceful, filter-feeds from the water. Lives among anemones.

595.comportamento: Peaceful, carries anemones in its claws like "boxing gloves".
595.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 20 liters. One of the most fascinating invertebrates.

596.comportamento: Peaceful, attaches pieces of coral and algae to its body as camouflage.

597.comportamento: Peaceful, nocturnal, feeds on detritus. Part of the clean-up crew.

598.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 80 liters. Reef safe.

599.comportamento: Peaceful, difficult to keep, requires a mature tank.
599.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 200 liters. Acclimation of 3+ hours required.

600.comportamento: Peaceful, burrows into the sand and cleans the substrate.

601.comportamento: Coral predator! DO NOT keep in a reef tank. Venomous spines.
601.outrasInformacoes: NOT recommended for aquariums. A pest of natural reefs.

602.comportamento: Peaceful, excellent algae eater. Has long, thin spines.
602.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank size: 100 liters. Spines can pierce skin.

603.comportamento: Peaceful, has thick pencil-like spines. Eats algae and detritus.

604.comportamento: Peaceful, blue and black striped. Eats algae. Reef safe.
604.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank: 60 liters. One of the safest urchins for reef tanks.

605.comportamento: Peaceful, devours algae quickly. Essential cleanup crew member.

606.comportamento: Peaceful, eats algae on glass and rocks. Cannot right itself when overturned.

607.comportamento: Peaceful, lives buried in sand, emerges to eat leftover food.
607.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank: 20 liters. Cleans the substrate.

608.comportamento: Peaceful, eats algae and detritus in sandy substrate.

609.comportamento: Peaceful, eats algae including diatoms. Can right itself when overturned.
609.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank: 30 liters. Superior to the Astrea snail.

610.comportamento: Peaceful, eats algae and cyanobacteria in the substrate. Expressive eyes.

611.comportamento: Peaceful, photosynthetic. Stunning colors under actinic lighting.
611.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank: 100 liters. Strong lighting is mandatory.

612.comportamento: Intelligent, escape artist, predator. Dedicated tank with a secure lid required.
612.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank: 80 liters. Invertebrate pet, interacts with its owner.

613.comportamento: Peaceful, filters the substrate. Can release toxins when stressed.
613.outrasInformacoes: Minimum tank: 150 liters. Caution: can poison the aquarium.

615.comportamento: Peaceful, feeds exclusively on aiptasia anemones (pest).
615.outrasInformacoes: Biological control for aiptasia. Dies once food source is depleted.

616.comportamento: Peaceful, requires a dedicated circular tank (kreisel). Hypnotic visual display.
616.outrasInformacoes: Minimum kreisel tank: 50 liters. Do NOT keep with fish.
